race.name: Lapponia Hiihto
race.tagline: One hundred and ninety kilometers across the fells of Finnish Lapland in three days — the longest multi-stage ski race in the Nordic world.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple stations per stage with warm drinks and traditional Finnish fare

climate.description: April in western Lapland brings lengthening days but still-cold temperatures. Race conditions typically range from -15°C to 0°C. Late-season sun can soften snow dramatically by afternoon, creating challenging wax conditions across the three days.
challenges: Three consecutive days of ultra-distance skiing
Dramatic temperature swings between morning and afternoon stages
Late-season sun creating wet, grabby snow conditions
Remote fell terrain with limited shelter from wind
Cumulative fatigue across 190km demands careful pacing

course.primary: Three-day stage race traversing the fells of western Finnish Lapland from Hetta to Muonio. The course crosses frozen lakes, climbs over treeline fell plateaus, and descends through Arctic birch forests. Each stage averages over 60km with significant cumulative elevation.
course.surface: Machine-groomed tracks through fell wilderness, frozen lakes, and birch forest
course.grooming: Good Finnish standard, prepared for both classic and skate
features: Longest multi-stage ski race in the Nordic countries at 190km total
Crosses the fell wilderness between Hetta and Muonio
Frozen lake crossings with panoramic fell views

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: An extraordinary ultra-distance stage race in one of Europe's most remote corners. The 190km total distance and fell terrain are unmatched, but the remote location limits field size and accessibility.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Prestige 4 promotes from T3 to T2

history.summary: Founded in 1978, Lapponia Hiihto was conceived as a multi-day wilderness crossing that would showcase the vast fell landscape of western Finnish Lapland. The route from Hetta to Muonio traverses some of the most remote terrain in Finland. The race joined the Ski Classics Challengers series, bringing international attention to this unique stage-race format.
notable_facts: At 190km over three days, it is the longest multi-stage XC ski race in Nordic countries
The route crosses the traditional homeland of the Sámi people
Hetta (Enontekiö) and Muonio are among Finland's northernmost municipalities
Part of the Ski Classics Challengers series
Snow conditions in April Lapland are among the most reliable in the world
The fell landscape is designated as a UNESCO biosphere reserve area

videos.description: Videolla Tervatar Maija Oravamäki esittelee Tervahiihtoa 2018. Videon tuotti Lasse Hurmalainen.

videos.title: Finlandia-hiihto - 22nd February 2025
videos.description: As always, the first race day of the weekend was skied with a classic style. The longest route was 62 km (38.5 miles) long. All starting groups and some moments from finish line are shown in this video.

videos.title: Muonio TV,  Lapponia Ski Week with Okko Kinnunen
videos.description:
This is the second interview with my former winter work college who took part in this year's Lapponia ski week.

We hope you enjoy this video, please leave any comments and suggestions. Questions can be send to muoniotv@gmail.com

www.muoniotv.com

See you soon for the next video.

videos.curation_reason: First-person interview recap of participating in Lapponia Ski Week with detailed race experiences and conditions.

videos.title: Best Moments of Lapponia Ski Week 2023
videos.description:
The Ski Classics Challengers event Lapponia Ski Week took place last week with a three-day stage race in freestyle technique in Finland. 

Now you can enjoy the event highlights!

Video Credits: Anssi Mäkinen, www.anselmus.net
